text: Welcome to Broadcom Inc. 2nd Quarter fiscal year,

text: 2026 Financial Results Conference Call.

text: head of Investor Relations of Broadcom Inc.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you, operator, and good afternoon, everyone.

text: and ROM, Volaga, President, Infrastructure, Software Group.

speaker: KIRSTEN SPEARS - CFO

text: Amy Teener. Thank you, Kirsten, for your leadership

text: over the past 12 years. Broadcom distributed a press release

text: and financial tables after the market closed,

text: 2026. If you did not receive a copy,

text: you may obtain the information from the Investor section of Broadcom's
speaker: OPERATOR

text: website at Broadcom.com.

text: This conference call is being webcast live,

text: and an audio replay of the call can be accessed for one year

text: through the Investor section of Broadcom's website.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: During the prepared comments, Hochen Kirsten will be providing details of our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: second quarter fiscal year 2026 results, guidance for our third quarter fiscal
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year 2026, as well as commentary regarding the business
speaker: OPERATOR

text: environment. We'll take questions after the end
speaker: OPERATOR

text: of our prepared comments. Please refer to our press release today
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and our recent filings with the SEC for information on risk factors that could
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cause our actual results to differ materially from the forward-looking
speaker: OPERATOR

text: statements made on this call. In addition to U.S. gap reporting,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Broadcom reports certain financial measures on a non-gap basis.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: A reconciliation between gap and non-gap measures to the extent possible
speaker: OPERATOR

text: is included in the tables attached to today's press release.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Comments made during today's call will primarily refer to our non-gap
speaker: OPERATOR

text: financial results. I will now turn the call over to Hoc.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you, Jay. Thank you, everyone, for attending and joining today.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: In our fiscal Q2 2026 total revenue reach a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: record 22.2 billion dollars. Up 48 percent year on year,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: above our guidance on strength in AI semiconductors.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Q2 operating margin was a record 67 percent and adjusted
speaker: OPERATOR

text: EBITDA was a record 69 percent of revenue, which was above our guidance.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Even as our revenue scales are massively driven by AI,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: our operating and EBITDA margins remain strong and stable.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Turning to semiconductors Q2 revenue was a record 15 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: As I said before, as we grew 79 percent year on year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Driving this growth was AI semiconductor revenue at a record 10.8
speaker: OPERATOR

text: billion, up 143 percent year on year and above our outlook.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Networking represented almost 40 percent of our Q2 AI revenue.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Demand for XPUs and networking is simply insatiable.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: During the quarter, bookings for AI semiconductors
speaker: OPERATOR

text: were over 30 billion dollars against the 10.8 billion
speaker: OPERATOR

text: dollars we shipped. In the second half of 2026,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we expect AI semiconductor revenue to double from the first half we shipped
speaker: OPERATOR

text: last from the ship this year. Consistent with this trend in Q3,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we expect AI semiconductor revenue to accelerate to 16 billion dollars
speaker: OPERATOR

text: up over 22 up over 200 percent year on year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For the full year 2026, we expect to achieve a two-achieve AI semiconductor
speaker: OPERATOR

text: revenue of 56 billion dollars up approximately 180 percent
speaker: OPERATOR

text: from fiscal 2025. Now we expect this momentum to continue
speaker: OPERATOR

text: into fiscal year 2027 and reiterate our AI semiconductor revenue guidance
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to be in excess of 100 billion dollars.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We expect AI semiconductor revenue growth to continue
speaker: OPERATOR

text: in fiscal 2028 based on the following initiatives we have with our six
speaker: OPERATOR

text: core customers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: As you are aware, we've Google, we announced in April that we entered into a long-term
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agreement to develop and supply multiple generations of TPUs
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and AI networking. Our relationship continues to be strategic
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and very substantial as we continue to deliver
speaker: OPERATOR

text: vastly superior technology and execution compared to other alternatives.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: This ability to provide differentiated value to Google ensures that our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: business will sustain and grow for the foreseeable future.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For Entropic, as you know for 2026, we are providing access to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Broadcom TPU base compute of over one gigawatts.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: In April, we entered into an agreement to enable Entropic to access
speaker: OPERATOR

text: another five gigawatts of next-generation TPU base compute
speaker: OPERATOR

text: beginning in 2027.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For OpenAI, we have delivered silicon and we are on
speaker: OPERATOR

text: train for production late 2026. We have a contractual commitment to deploy
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 1.3 gigawatts in 2027 as part of the larger 10 gigawatts
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that by 2029 agreement we announced last year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For method in April, we announced a partnership to deliver multiple
speaker: OPERATOR

text: generations of MTIA XPUs and under this agreement
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we expect to deploy three gigawatts through the end of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 2028. The initial order for one gigawatt which includes
speaker: OPERATOR

text: XPUs and our networking has been received and will start delivery
speaker: OPERATOR

text: in the second half of 2027. For our other two customers,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we expect shipments to begin late 2026 and accelerate into 2027.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: To date, we have received purchase orders totaling $6 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: While we have significant IP and execution leadership in XPUs,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: networking is key to building scalable XPU and GPU clusters.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And here in networking, we have at least one generation of technology
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and product leadership. For scale-up within RACS, we enable
speaker: OPERATOR

text: direct attach copper based on an industry leading 200G
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and 400 gig cities, driving called package copper
speaker: OPERATOR

text: with internet and PCX press switches. For scale-out between RACS,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we have been shipping the industry only 100 terabit
speaker: OPERATOR

text: internet switch, the TAMON 6, for over a year. We will now be
speaker: OPERATOR

text: tapping out our next generation 200 terabit switch
speaker: OPERATOR

text: this quarter and in CPOs, which is called package optics,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 1.6 terabit DSPs, CW and EML lasers, we are the de facto
speaker: OPERATOR

text: standard in the industry. To extend AI clusters across data centers,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we remain the industry leader with our Jericho 3 and Jericho 4 fabric solutions
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and enabling the world's largest deployments of multiple hyperscalers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our strategic vision is to bring together Broadcom's leading technology
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and investor partners with the strongest balance sheets to deliver at scale
speaker: OPERATOR

text: sufficient compute capacity at the lowest cost and power for the leading
speaker: OPERATOR

text: AI frontier labs, including entropic and open AI. To deliver this vision,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we are creating the AI XTV platform with Apollo and Blackstone and other leading investors
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to deploy more than 20 gigawatts of compute capacity through 2028.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The first trench of this platform, valued at $35 billion, is in fact currently been launched by
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Apollo. Now turning to non-AI semiconductors, Q2 revenue of $4.2 billion was up 6% year on year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Bookings during the same period exceeded $6 billion, which is a clear indication way on the path
speaker: OPERATOR

text: towards a full cyclical recovery. Broadband, service storage and enterprise net working together
speaker: OPERATOR

text: were up partially offset by a seasonal decline in wireless. Consistent with this trend in Q3,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we forecast non-AI semiconductor revenue to be approximately $4.5 billion, up 12% from a year ago.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: In summary, we expect Q3 semiconductor revenue to be $20.5 billion, up 124% year on year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Let me turn to infrastructure software segment. Q2 software revenue of $7.2 billion was up 9%
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year on year, in line with our guidance. Bookings continue to be strong as we sustain ARR growth
speaker: OPERATOR

text: of 17% year over year. For Q3, we forecast software revenue to be approximately $8.9 billion, up 31%
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year on year. We just released VMware Cloud Foundation 9.1, focused on improving infrastructure
speaker: OPERATOR

text: efficiency, security and support for enterprise AI inferencing workloads. With strong server-demand
speaker: OPERATOR

text: globally, the deployment of VCF 9.1 for on-prem cloud computing is extremely strong, driving
speaker: OPERATOR

text: robust revenue growth. This release adds heterogeneous compute support across GPUs
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and CPU architectures, including AMD Intel and NVIDIA platforms, enabling customers, enterprise
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customers to run AI, Kubernetes and traditional virtualized workloads on a common private cloud
speaker: OPERATOR

text: environment. To sum it up, for Q3, 2026, we expect our consolidated revenue to grow to 29.4 billion,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: up 84% year on year. We expect operating margin to be stable at approximately 67% of revenue
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and adjusted EBITDA to be at approximately 68% of revenue.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And we then let me turn the call over to Kiazza.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you, Hawk. Let me now provide additional detail on our Q2 financial performance.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Consolidated revenue with a record 22.2 billion for the quarter, up 48% from a year ago.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Gross margin was 77.1% of revenue in the quarter, down 230 basis points year on year,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: a semiconductor became a larger proportion of our product mix.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Consolidated operating expenses were 2.2 billion, of which 1.6 billion was R&D. Q2 operating income
speaker: OPERATOR

text: was a record 14.9 billion, up 52% from a year ago. Note that even with the decline in gross margin,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: operating margin increased 200 basis points year over year to 67.3% as operating expenses
speaker: OPERATOR

text: remained relatively flat. Adjusted EBITDA, a 15.2 billion or 69% of revenue was above our guidance of 68%.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Now a review of the P&L for our two segments, starting with semiconductors.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Revenue for our semiconductor solution segment was a record 15 billion, with growth accelerating
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to 79% year on year driven by AI. Semiconductor revenue represented 68% of total revenue in the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: quarter, and AI semiconductor revenue represented 49% of total revenue. Gross margin for our semiconductor
speaker: OPERATOR

text: solution segment was approximately 70%. Operating expenses of 1.2 billion reflected increased
speaker: OPERATOR

text: investment in R&D for leading edge AI semiconductors and represented 8% of revenue. Semiconductor
speaker: OPERATOR

text: operating margin of 62% was up 460 basis points year on year reflecting our strong operating
speaker: OPERATOR

text: leverage. Now moving on to infrastructure software. Revenue for infrastructure software of 7.2 billion
speaker: OPERATOR

text: was up 9% year on year and represented 32% of revenue. Gross margin for infrastructure software
speaker: OPERATOR

text: was 93% in the quarter and operating expenses were 1 billion in the quarter. Q2 software operating
speaker: OPERATOR

text: margin was up 310 basis points year on year to approximately 79%.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Moving on to cash flow. Free cash flow in the quarter was a record 10.3 billion and represented
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 46% of revenue. We spent 231 million on capital expenditures. We ended the second quarter with 19.6
speaker: OPERATOR

text: billion of cash compared to 14.2 billion in the prior quarter. We ended the second quarter with
speaker: OPERATOR

text: inventory of 4.3 billion as we continued to secure supply to support strong AI demand. Our days of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: inventory on hand were 86 days in Q2 compared to 68 days in Q1 in anticipation of accelerating
speaker: OPERATOR

text: AI semiconductor growth in the second half of the year. Turning to capital allocation. In Q2 we paid
speaker: OPERATOR

text: stockholders 3.1 billion of cash dividends based on a quarterly common stock cash dividend of 65
speaker: OPERATOR

text: cents per share. Now moving to guidance. Our guidance for Q3 is for consolidated revenue of 29.4
speaker: OPERATOR

text: billion up 84% year on year. We forecast semiconductor revenue of approximately 20.5 billion up 124%
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year on year. Within this we expect Q3 AI semiconductor revenue of 16 billion up over 200% year on year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We expect Q3 infrastructure software revenue of approximately 8.9 billion up 31% year on year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Moving on to margins. As the proportion of AI revenue significantly grows in Q3 we expect Q3
speaker: OPERATOR

text: consolidated gross margin to be down to approximately 74%. This decline in gross margin does not
speaker: OPERATOR

text: represent a structural change in semiconductor margin rather it reflects product mix between
speaker: OPERATOR

text: semiconductors and infrastructure software. Regardless of the impact to gross margin we expect Q3
speaker: OPERATOR

text: operating margin to be 67% which is flat quarter on quarter demonstrating our strong operating leverage.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We highly recommend that investors model semiconductor and infrastructure software margins
speaker: OPERATOR

text: separately to properly reflect the impact of changes in total revenue mix going forward.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We expect the non-gap tax rate for Q3 in fiscal year 2026 to be approximately 16%
speaker: OPERATOR

text: due to the impact of the global minimum tax and the geographic mix of income compared to that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: of fiscal year 25. In Q3 we expect the non-gap diluted share count to be approximately 4.94 billion
speaker: OPERATOR

text: shares excluding the impact of potential share repurchases. That concludes my prepared remarks.
speaker: OPERATOR
